title: Bridge Essay: Minimal self-consciousness and the flying man argument.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nondual/Unity** (Phase 16) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Nondual/Unity to mechanistic neuroscience through Minimal, self-consciousness, and, the, flying.

**Minimal self-consciousness and the flying man argument.** — Front Psychol. PMID: 38169988. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2023.1296656. The concept of minimal self-consciousness or "minimal self" is equivalent to avery basic form of first-person, pre-reflective self-awareness, which includes bodily self-awareness, and is related to phenomenal experience (qualia) and sentience. This phenomenological concept plays arole in characterizations of the senses of ownership and agency; in recent debates about Buddhist conceptions of the no-self; in explanations of illusions such as the Rubber Hand Illusion; as well as in characterizati
